pundit.name: Landon Donovan
pundit.take: **I feel for Devin, I really do, but when you draft Haiti in the fourth round, I mean... what conversation were you having with yourself?** Look, I get why Devin thought Spain was a solid choice as a T1 team, given their storied history, but when your other picks include the U.S. and Norway, it's like betting on a horse race with a unicorn. Bless his heart, at least Devin has a dreamer's spirit. As for Zach, aka "Mustard Boy," he might have thought drafting Austria was clever, but let’s be honest—Austria's form has been more lukewarm than the side of ketchup at a hot dog stand. I'm absurdly confident Spain will outclass Austria in Toronto, not because Devin's strategic mind shines, but more because Austria isn't likely to be the surprise of the tournament Zach envisioned.

pundit_takes.headline: Ghost Pepper Devin and Mustard Boy Zach Set for Fiery Duel
pundit_takes.subtitle: Devin relies on Spain's sizzle, while Zach hopes Austria's spice won't fizzle.

pundit_takes.headline: Gayden's England Triumphs Over Bubba G's DR Congo Without Upset Points
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gayden expertly avoids upsets by letting DR Congo lose as expected.

pundit_takes.headline: Mustard Boy Zach Hoping for a Smooth Spread with Switzerland
pundit_takes.subtitle: Zach dreams of an easy match-up where Swiss precision meets Algerian chaos.

pundit_takes.headline: Undrafted Nations Poised to Outshine Fantasy Giants at World Cup
pundit_takes.subtitle: Portugal and Croatia aim to prove managers are no talent scouts.

rome_analytics_quotes: Rafe's Germany just got knocked out—same story, different day for the teenage chaos agent.
Gunner holds on top with a Tier-4 beast, while Rafe drafts in his sleep.
Rafe's 6-point differential against Devin is a family-friendly demolition job.
Teen novice Rafe disrupts Gunner's odds—guess the sim loves a wild card.
